purpose: fail-closed runtime control contract for controller-heavy self-steering, artifact-grounded latent-intent adjudication, and recurrence prevention before skill promotion

candidate_directions.description: Competing execution directions considered before committing to a runtime plan.

chosen_direction.description: The direction that becomes the runtime primary control surface for the current turn.

rejected_directions.description: Discarded directions with explicit rejection reasons.

kill_conditions.description: Conditions that force direction invalidation or stop further retries.

current_gap.description: Current delta between the chosen direction and adoption-ready output.

self_correction_applied.description: Corrections already applied before asking the user for more input.

candidate_intent_hypotheses.description: Competing latent-intent hypotheses tied to adoption outcomes.

chosen_intent_model.description: Selected latent-intent model that steers planning and evaluation.

benchmark_strengths_to_surpass.description: Named strengths from the relevant benchmark or reference surface that the artifact must intentionally beat.

artifact_comparison_evidence.description: Artifact-grounded comparison evidence demonstrating why the chosen intent model is adoptable.

next_turn_recurrence_patch.description: Immediate next-turn prevention patch generated from the current miss or correction.

recurrence_patch_decision.description: Decision record explaining why the recurrence patch is the smallest scope that prevents recurrence.

notes: reporting artifacts do not substitute for runtime primary control state
latent intent cannot pass on proxy score alone
immediate recurrence prevention must exist before any skill promotion decision can pass

acceptanceChecks.ac-1: self-steering state is contractually defined as the runtime primary control surface, not a reporting-only summary
acceptanceChecks.ac-2: latent intent requires artifact-grounded comparison evidence rather than proxy score alone
acceptanceChecks.ac-3: next-turn recurrence patch is required before skill promotion and must be package-visible in verification
